steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for an opening to a narration about Wulffa maculata’s collection. The opening must focus on that specific specimen — not comparisons with other plants or general information about Mexía’s broader work.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — narrates the specific moment Mexía collected Wulffa maculata: gives the date, location, and family classification, anchoring that specimen as the clear center of the narrative. ✓
  • Choice B — introduces a second specimen: shifts focus away from Wulffa maculata to a different plant. ✗
  • Choice C — reframes the specimen as a comparison: makes another plant the reference point rather than centering Wulffa maculata. ✗
  • Choice D — discusses where specimens are now stored: moves attention to present-day storage rather than the moment of collection. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
